title: Phaleria urens
taxonomy: Phaleria urens is classified within the family Thymelaeaceae. It belongs to the genus Phaleria and is a species of flowering plant. The classification for Phaleria urens is Phaleria urens Thunb. It is native to the tropical regions of Asia and Australia. Phaleria urens is a shrub or small tree, commonly known as the 'Stinking Passion Fruit' due to its strong unpleasant odor.
uses: Phaleria urens has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties. It is believed to have anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal effects, which may help in treating skin conditions and wounds. The plant is also used in traditional medicine to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs. Additionally, Phaleria urens has been studied for its potential anti-cancer properties and antioxidant effects. Further research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal uses and potential benefits.
benefits: Phaleria urens is known for its various health benefits, which include reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. The plant has antimicrobial properties, making it effective in treating skin infections and wounds. It also has antifungal properties, which help in preventing fungal infections. The plant is also used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever and respiratory issues. side_effects: Phaleria urens is known to be associated with certain side effects, including gastrointestinal issues, allergic reactions, and interactions with other medications.
